qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable, and how is it used to integrate local module trees with the central CVMFS software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The `RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS` environment variable allows you to define locations for local module trees, which will automatically integrate into the central CVMFS module tree. To use it, you define the variable, for example, `export RSNT_LOCAL_MODULEPATHS=/opt/software/easybuild/modules`, and then install your EasyBuild recipes using a command like `eb --installpath /opt/software/easybuild <your recipe>.eb`.

qa_pairs.question: When working with the CVMFS software environment, how can a user add a local path to a flat module tree and specify its priority?
qa_pairs.answer: If you want to add a local path to a flat module tree, you should add the `module use` command after sourcing the CVMFS scripts. To give your module tree higher priority than the central CVMFS modules, use `module use --priority 1 /path/to/your/flat/module/tree`. To give it lower priority, use `module use --priority -1 /path/to/your/flat/module/tree`.
